stem: A education study measures average test score improvement across 4 schools, each containing multiple classrooms. school A has 20 observations with mean 61.3 points (within-school SD = 6.6). school B has 20 observations with mean 50.5 points (within-school SD = 4.1). school C has 10 observations with mean 56.7 points (within-school SD = 4.9). school D has 5 observations with mean 45.0 points (within-school SD = 4.5). The between-school standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 6.4 and 15.9 points. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true average test score improvement for school A, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-school variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for school A: 61.2 points (shrunk from raw 61.3). Range [61.0, 61.2] as between-school SD varies from 6.4 to 15.9. Pooled mean: 55.1.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 55.1 points (weighted average of 4 school means).
Shrinkage factor for school A: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/20). At σ_between=10.6: B = 0.981.
Interval [61.0, 61.2] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (61.3) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (55.1) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
